CIVIC CENTER PLAZA
201 No(tr| Broad! ay. Escondiro
Cslitomia 92025-2798
(61 9) 74r -1608
FAX (619) 741 -75,i1
March25,7992
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 9580&0807
Re: Request for Advice
I am writing this letter pursuant to Government Code $83114 to request an
opinion on whether a particular legal entity is subiect to the Political Reform Act.
In the early 1980's local groups advocated construction of facilities to provide a
conference and meeting center for residents and visitors, and cultural facilities
for the performing and fine arts. The Escondido Civic Center Master Plan was
approved by the City Council in cooperation with the Civic Center Committee,
which was composed of Escondido citizens. In a brochure distributed in 1985, it
was noted that public participation was a key ingredient in the approval Process.
Indeed, the City Council placed the decision whether or not the Civic Center
Project should go forward on the ballot for a vote by the people in fune of 1985,
where it was approved.
The Center for the Ars Escondido is a California nonprofit corporation
incorporated on October 17, 1989. The Center for the Arts, Escondido
incorporated for the purpose of operating and managing the arts, theatre, and
conference center approved in the City of Escondido by the voters in 1985. The
arts, theatre, and conference c€nter are presently under conskuction and
completion is expected during 199&1994. The projected cost of the facilities is
$73.4 million. These monies have come from tax increment revenue generated
by the Escondido Redevelopment Area. The Escnndido Community
Development Commission is overseeing construction of the facilities.
The Center for the Arts Escondido is governed by a Board of Trustees. The
Articles and Bylaws of the Center for the Arts provide for a Board consisting of
twenty-one (21) persons. Twelve (12) of the Trustees are to be appointed by the
Escondido City Council, and the remaining nine (9) Trustees are to be selected
by the other members of the Board of Trustees. Two Escondido City Council
members sit as Trustees. At the direction of the Community Development
JERFIY C. HARMON, MAYOFI CAFLA DeDOMlNlClS, MAYOFI PRO TEM 
KRIS MURPHY SID HOLLINS RICHAFID A. FOSTER
Pnnied on Recycl6d Paper


Mardr25,7992
Page2
Commission, the Commission's general counsel prepared documents and
handled incorporation. Copies of the Bylaws and Articles of Incorporation are
attached.
Operating expenses of the Center for the Arts Escondido are funded primarily
by private donations at the pres€nt time, with event€enerated revenue expected
when consEuction of the Center is crcmplete. In addition, the City of Escondido
placed $2.29 million in trust in 1989, with the direction that the principal remain
in trust and income be used for operating exPenses. At Present, the average
interest income is $150,000.00. The City has also budgeted an amount not to
exceed $500,000 from the City's general fund toward the Center's oPerating costs
each year.
The annual operating budget of The Center will be approdmately $4,000,000 in
the next two or three years, making the City's contribution less than one-eighth
of the total.
As a result of a recent amendment to 56252(b) of the California Government
Code (Stats 1991, Chapter 181), The Center is not subject to the Public Records
Act.
The Center for the Arts nonprofit corporation is not only in charge of operating
and managing the arts, theatre, and conference c€nter, but also has replaced the
Felicita Foundation, a completely private entity providing cultural and arts
activities in the city for a number of years.
It has been the objective of the City Council of the City of Escondido that the
Center no, be run on an ongoing basis by the City. lndeed, the City Council
desires to have a separate Board and substantial private interests involved in the
operations of the Center as much as possible.
The question on which your assistance is requested is whether or not the
Trustees are subject to the requirements of the Political Reform Act ol 7974,
Governrnent Code $81 0G91 01 5.
Please let me know if you would like to discuss this issue further. As well, I can
provide any particular facts you may find necessary to your consideration of this
matter.

sections.question: The question on which your assistance is requested is whether or not the Trustees are subject to the requirements of the Political Reform Act of 1974, Government Code §81006-91015.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Trustees of the Center for the Arts Escondido, a nonprofit corporation incorporated to operate arts and conference facilities approved by voters, are subject to the Political Reform Act of 1974.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from City Attorney to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections but contains a clear legal question at the end. Some text is partially illegible due to scan quality. No FPPC advice/conclusion is present in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: The question on which your assistance is requested is whether or not the Trustees are subject to the requirements of the Political Reform Act of 1974, Government Code §81006-91015.

embedding.summary: The City Attorney of Escondido requests FPPC advice on whether Trustees of the Center for the Arts Escondido nonprofit corporation are subject to Political Reform Act requirements. The Center operates facilities funded primarily by private donations and public trust funds, with a Board containing appointed city council members and independently selected trustees.
